name: Michael Lee
tagline: M.S. Analytics · Georgia Tech  |  B.S. Information Science · UMD
bio: I build things end-to-end — from data pipelines to deployed SaaS products. Most effective at the intersection of engineering, data, and business strategy: I don't just write code, I ship products that solve real problems. Background in USAF; focused on data engineering, federal, and defense roles.

projects.name: ReviewStar
projects.problem: Job seekers practice behavioral interviews without structured feedback — generic advice doesn't help them improve at the STAR level.
projects.evidence: Built full-stack SaaS: FastAPI backend, Next.js frontend (static export), Supabase auth + PostgreSQL, Google OAuth, mic input (Web Speech API) or text. LLaMA 3.3 70B via Groq scores each STAR section and rewrites answers. Three-tier limits (anonymous: 3, free logged-in: 10/mo, Pro: unlimited). Stripe live subscriptions, session history dashboard for Pro users. Deployed on AWS EC2 Ubuntu with systemd.
projects.result: Live in production at reviewstar.cv. Free and Pro ($9/mo) tiers with paying subscribers.

projects.name: ClearDeath — Funeral Home Case Manager
projects.problem: A local funeral home needed digital case management but couldn't rely on cloud SaaS — staff work in-office without guaranteed internet access.
projects.evidence: Built a self-hosted local web app: Next.js 16 + React 19 frontend, PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM, NextAuth v5 for staff authentication (bcrypt), Docker Compose deployment on a Windows mini PC. Staff access via office WiFi (local IP) — no internet required after setup. Weekly automated backups via Docker tar.gz.
projects.result: Deployed on a single mini PC, accessible to all staff on the local network. Full case management, multi-user auth, and automated backup — zero recurring SaaS cost.

projects.name: AWS IAM Lifecycle Pipeline
projects.problem: Manual IAM management in enterprise environments creates compliance gaps — stale accounts linger after offboarding and privilege creep goes undetected.
projects.evidence: Automated joiner-mover-leaver IAM workflow: Terraform-provisioned infra, MFA enforcement via policy condition (`aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent`), least-privilege role assignment, CloudTrail audit logging, IAM Access Analyzer integration, dry-run mode, and full pytest coverage. Users are never deleted — deprovisioned tag preserves audit trail for AC-2 compliance.
projects.result: Production-ready pipeline satisfying NIST 800-53 and CMMC Level 2 controls. Full per-change audit trail; eliminates manual access-review lag.

projects.name: AI Labor Market Risk Pipeline
projects.problem: No model existed to correlate AI-exposure risk with financial vulnerability across occupations — making it hard to assess credit risk in AI-disrupted labor markets.
projects.evidence: End-to-end pipeline: Python ingestion from BLS OEWS, Anthropic Economic Index, O*NET, and Census ACS PUMS; Terraform-provisioned AWS S3 + IAM; loaded to Snowflake via boto3; 43 dbt tests across staging → intermediate → mart layers. Composite risk score: 0.40 × AI exposure + 0.60 × financial vulnerability. Solved 15 real integration bugs (Census URL migration, BLS 403 errors, Snowflake load history, O*NET column mapping).
projects.result: MART_CREDIT_RISK_BY_OCCUPATION ranks every occupation by composite risk score (top: Medical Transcriptionists at 0.847). Full data quality coverage across all mart models.

projects.name: DoD Small Business Contract Pipeline
projects.problem: Federal contract data from USASpending.gov is large, paginated, and raw — no easy way to track DoD small business trends or benchmark vendors.
projects.evidence: End-to-end ETL: paginated REST API ingestion → pandas transforms (dedup, negative-amount filter, NAICS classification, 80% pass-rate validation) → PostgreSQL via SQLAlchemy 2.0 + pipeline audit log. Interactive Streamlit + Plotly dashboard with KPI cards, spending by NAICS sector, top-15 recipients, choropleth map, and sidebar filters. Extended to Azure Blob Storage, Data Factory, and Azure SQL.
projects.result: Fully functional analytics platform over federal contract data — usable for vendor research and competitive landscape analysis.

projects.name: Job Agent
projects.problem: Manually checking dozens of career pages and USAJobs daily is slow, inconsistent, and easy to miss relevant postings.
projects.evidence: Playwright scraper targeting Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, and USAJobs API; SQLite deduplication; Claude API relevance scoring against resume + profile summary (min score threshold configurable); Gmail App Password HTML email digest — all on a daily cron schedule.
projects.result: Runs every morning, surfacing only relevant opportunities scored above threshold. Removes the manual search loop entirely.

projects.name: Crypto Spam Tracker
projects.problem: YouTube comment sections for crypto content are flooded with scam promotions — no easy way to monitor which coins are being targeted in real time.
projects.evidence: FastAPI backend with APScheduler polling the YouTube Comments API every 7 minutes; pattern-matching detector extracts crypto coin mentions and classifies spam; SQLite stores comment content, coin, and timestamp; live price lookup via prices.py; plain-JS dashboard with per-coin filtering and Windows desktop notifications.
projects.result: Running monitoring service tracking crypto spam patterns across YouTube with a live web dashboard showing comment content, timestamps, and coin-level breakdown.

projects.name: Game Sales Data Analysis
projects.problem: Global video game sales data from CSV exports had no structure for meaningful querying or trend analysis.
projects.evidence: Designed a normalized MySQL relational database with 6 tables (Games, Platforms, Publishers, Regions, Year, Sales); cleaned ~2,000 raw entries; wrote SQL queries with joins, aggregations, and subqueries to surface trends by platform, region, and genre.
projects.result: Identified high-performing titles and emerging market trends. Demonstrated the full data modeling lifecycle from raw CSV to analytical query.

projects.name: iConsultancy: Nonprofit Data Analysis
projects.problem: A nonprofit had inconsistent, uncleaned donor and pickup-request data with no framework for data-driven operational decisions.
projects.evidence: Cleaned and standardized multi-year donor and pickup datasets; built donor segmentation analysis; created user journey maps from stakeholder interviews; identified data governance and privacy gaps; delivered dashboards and structured presentations to leadership.
projects.result: Translated qualitative and quantitative findings into actionable service workflow improvements, directly supporting strategic planning decisions.

projects.name: Threat Intelligence Briefs
projects.problem: Security teams need concise, actionable threat intelligence — raw CVE advisories are too dense to act on quickly.
projects.evidence: Authored 10+ threat briefs analyzing real-world CVE incidents; mapped adversary TTPs to the MITRE ATT&CK framework; outlined mitigations aligned with NIST and federal guidance for each incident.
projects.result: Produced structured, decision-ready intelligence reports covering the full incident-to-mitigation chain across a range of CVEs and threat actors.

projects.name: Internet Systems Architecture & Simulation
projects.problem: Designing secure networks requires hands-on configuration experience beyond what theory alone provides.
projects.evidence: Simulated full internet architecture in Cisco Packet Tracer — reconfigured IP/DHCP/NAT/routing tables, set up DNS and HTTP servers, established VPN tunnels with encrypted comms validation, edited IoT and API server behavior, traced RIP/RIPng/IoT packets, and monitored an SDN dashboard.
projects.result: Demonstrated end-to-end secure network design from Layer 2 to application layer, including IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack and IoT integration across simulated enterprise topology.

projects.name: USG iSchool Chatbot
projects.problem: An in-progress university chatbot lacked alignment between stakeholder expectations, student needs, and actual response quality.
projects.evidence: Joined mid-project; partnered with advisors, IT, and departmental stakeholders to define success criteria; analyzed the RAG-based LLM architecture to improve context structuring; moderated quality using like/dislike feedback signals to iteratively refine response guidelines.
projects.result: Improved chatbot accuracy and relevance through structured feedback loops and better context management, aligned with real student-facing use cases.

projects.name: Smart Pet Litter Box Redesign
projects.problem: Existing smart litter box products had persistent usability and maintenance pain points that competitors hadn't adequately addressed.
projects.evidence: Led end-to-end product discovery: user research, competitive analysis, problem statement and persona definition, feature prioritization, low-fidelity prototyping for cleaning and odor control workflows, and a phased product roadmap with documented tradeoffs.
projects.result: Delivered a prioritized feature set and prototype targeting core user pain points, with a roadmap outlining phased improvements grounded in research.
